tema: Present Perfect — The Bucket List (already / yet)

banco_oraciones.oracion: I have already gotten a tattoo.
Sofía has already run half a marathon.
Kenji has already eaten hormigas culonas.
Abuela Carmen has done everything on her list.
I haven't written a book yet.
Ben hasn't shown the tattoo to his mum yet.
We haven't climbed a volcano yet.
Have you finished your bucket list yet?
What have you already done this year?
Have you gotten a tattoo? Yes, I have.
Has she written her book yet? No, she hasn't.
Have they flown in a helicopter? No, they haven't.

interaccion: Sofía: Bucket list check! I **have already run** a marathon. Well... half. OK, a quarter.
Ben: I **have already gotten** a tattoo — a tiny map of Colombia. I **haven't shown** it to my mum **yet**.
Kenji: I **have already eaten** hormigas culonas. I **haven't tried** mondongo **yet**. I need time.
Abuela Carmen: I **have done** everything on my list. So I **have started** a new list.
inicio.definicion_pragmatica: The present perfect (have + third form) talks about your life results up to now — perfect for a bucket list: what you have already done and what you haven't done yet.

filas: I have already gotten a tattoo
I haven't written a book yet
Have you finished your list yet?
Yes, I have. / No, she hasn't.
Have you tried it? Yes, I have.
at the end of negatives and questions

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'Already' goes with things you have done; 'yet' goes with pending things in negatives and questions.
The present perfect uses the second form of the verb: 'I have wrote'.

ejercicios.texto: ___ you finished your bucket list yet?

ejercicios.respuesta: I haven't written a book yet
She has already run a marathon

ejercicios.texto_con_error: I have saw that movie three times.
ejercicios.respuesta: I have seen that movie three times.

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Ben hasn't showed the tattoo to his mum yet.
ejercicios.respuesta: Ben hasn't shown the tattoo to his mum yet.

ejercicios.texto_con_error: I have already wrote two chapters.
ejercicios.respuesta: I have already written two chapters.
Has she finished her list yet?

ejercicios.oracion_base: I have already swum with dolphins.
ejercicios.respuesta: I haven't swum with dolphins yet.

ejercicios.oracion_base: They haven't flown in a helicopter yet.
ejercicios.respuesta: They have already flown in a helicopter.

ejercicios.pregunta: Have you eaten breakfast today? (short answer)
Have you climbed Everest? (short answer, No)

guion: Sofía: OK, team bucket list. Kenji, what have you already done in Colombia?
Kenji: I have already ridden a chiva, and I have already swum in the Caribbean.
Ben: Impressive. I haven't ridden a chiva yet. Is it safe?
Kenji: Ben, you have already fallen off a hammock. Nothing is safe for you.
Abuela Carmen: I have won three dance competitions, and I haven't finished yet.
Sofía: Abuela has already done more things than all of us together!

comprension.pregunta: What has Kenji already done in Colombia?
How many dance competitions has Abuela Carmen won?

reading.texto: Ben's bucket list is a small notebook with two columns. The left column says DONE: 'I have already gotten a tattoo (a tiny map of Colombia). I have already swum in two oceans. I have already eaten the spiciest taco in Mexico — and survived.' The right column says NOT YET: 'I haven't written my travel book yet, but I have already written the title: Clumsy Around the World. I haven't shown my tattoo to my mum yet. And I haven't learned to dance salsa yet — Abuela Carmen says there is no hope, but she has agreed to try one more time.'

preguntas.afirmacion: Ben has already gotten a tattoo.
He has already written his travel book.
He has already eaten a very spicy taco in Mexico.
Ben has already shown the tattoo to his mum.
